_note: ORIGINAL content. Topic coverage follows David Eck's Introduction to Programming Using Java. No prose, examples, or exercises were copied; the source guides curriculum order only.
title: Inheritance & Interfaces
summary: Share contracts and selected behavior without building rigid class trees.
refresher: A class `extends` one superclass and may `implement` several interfaces. Override methods to provide subtype behavior, use the interface type for flexible variables, and prefer composition when classes only need to reuse a helper rather than form a true is-a relationship.

blocks.body: Related classes often need the same operation but calculate it differently. An interface names that shared promise. Code can work with the promise while each class supplies its own implementation.

blocks.caption: Both classes satisfy Payable, so one list can hold both. The loop calls the version belonging to the actual object.

blocks.body: An interface can describe capability without forcing unrelated classes into one family tree. A class can implement several interfaces, but it can extend only one superclass.

blocks.caption: A method that receives the interface accepts any current or future implementation. Requiring one concrete class closes that door.

blocks.title: Inheritance means is-a
blocks.body: Use `extends` only when every child truly is a usable version of the parent. If one class merely needs another object's help, keep that helper in a field and use composition.

blocks.prompt: Which declaration lets a class promise the methods in Payable?
blocks.why: The `implements` keyword connects a class to an interface contract. The class must then provide the required abstract methods.

blocks.body: Overriding replaces inherited behavior for a subtype. Add `@Override` so the compiler checks that the method signature really matches instead of silently creating an unrelated overload.

choices: It asks the compiler to verify a matching parent method
blocks.why: The annotation catches misspelled names and mismatched parameters at compile time. It does not change access or create inheritance by itself.

blocks.title: Inheritance and interface checklist
items: Use an interface to state a capability.
Type variables by the contract when callers need flexibility.
Reserve `extends` for a true is-a relationship.
Prefer composition for simple code reuse.
